question: How do you configure Pebble log forwarding to Loki? Show the layer YAML and explain the protocol options.

claude_output.result:
Here's how to configure Pebble log forwarding to Loki:

## Layer YAML

```yaml
log-targets:
  my-loki-target:
    override: merge
    type: loki
    location: http://loki-host:3100/loki/api/v1/push
    services: [all]
    labels:
      env: production
      app: my-workload
```

## Key fields

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `override` | `merge` (combine with existing) or `replace` (fully override) |
| `type` | The protocol — see below |
| `location` | URL of the remote log receiver |
| `services` | List of services to forward. Use `[all]` for everything, or `-svcname` to exclude |
| `labels` | Optional key-value pairs attached to log entries. Supports `$ENV_VAR` substitution |

## Protocol options

Pebble supports three log forwarding protocols:

1. **`loki`** — Grafana Loki's HTTP push API. The location must be the full push endpoint, e.g. `http://host:3100/loki/api/v1/push`. Pebble automatically adds a `pebble_service` label identifying the source service.

2. **`otlp`** (OpenTelemetry) — Uses the OTLP HTTP protocol. Location is the base URL with port 4318, e.g. `http://host:4318`. Automatically sets `service.name` on log entries.

3. **`syslog`** — Standard syslog protocol. Location requires a transport prefix, e.g. `tcp://host:514`. Pebble uses the structured data ID `pebble@28978` to identify its messages.

## In a charm

In a Kubernetes charm, you'd typically set this up in your Pebble layer when handling a `loki-push-api` relation:

```python
def _pebble_layer(self) -> ops.pebble.LayerDict:
    return {
        "services": {
            "myworkload": {
                "override": "replace",
                "command": "/usr/bin/myworkload",
            },
        },
        "log-targets": {
            "loki": {
                "override": "replace",
                "type": "loki",
                "location": self._loki_push_url,  # from the relation
                "services": ["all"],
            },
        },
    }
```

The `services: [all]` directive forwards stdout/stderr from all Pebble-managed services to the target. You can selectively exclude services with a minus prefix (e.g. `[all, -noisy-svc]`).
